refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness (p95 ≤ 1.0 s) for operational use, ensuring data acquisition, processing, and dissemination meet latency targets.
refined_fr.rationale: To meet the overall latency requirement, the update process is decomposed into data acquisition, processing, and dissemination sub-functions, each contributing to the total latency budget.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end latency from data capture to worksite model update availability; verify each sub-function latency budget via simulation and field testing.
sub_frs: FR1: Acquire real-time worksite data with minimal delay
FR2: Process and update the worksite model within latency constraints
FR3: Disseminate updated worksite model to relevant modules promptly

candidate_dps: DP1: High-speed telemetry data acquisition with prioritized buffering
DP2: Real-time incremental model update algorithms optimized for low latency
DP3: Event-driven dissemination using efficient network protocols
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1, DP2, DP3 combined to address acquisition, processing, and dissemination respectively

potential_sources: Tight timing dependencies between acquisition and processing
coupling_check.notes: DPs are logically decoupled but require synchronized timing constraints; design must ensure minimal cross-domain interference.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network link supports required bandwidth and latency
Risk of telemetry data loss impacting model accuracy and latency
Processing algorithms must be optimized for worst-case scenarios
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Monitor telemetry network performance and consider fallback mechanisms for data loss.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system responsible for data acquisition, processing, and dissemination of worksite model updates

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time sensor and vehicle data for acquisition

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Disseminates updated worksite model to Vehicle and Teleoperation modules

module_containment_scores.notes: Centralizes update logic and data flow, minimizing diffusion and maximizing containment.
Primarily a data consumer; embedding update logic here would increase diffusion.
Focuses on control and tasking; not suitable for update processing.
Safety monitoring only; unrelated to update latency.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing update latency functionality within NGHE Solution module optimizes latency control and reduces cross-module dependencies.
containment_risks: Potential bottleneck if NGHE Solution module becomes overloaded
Single point of failure risk impacting update timeliness
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Consider modular internal partitioning within NGHE Solution to mitigate overload and improve fault tolerance.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DPs logically satisfy the refined FR sub-functions with clear latency focus and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk as update logic is centralized in NGHE Solution module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores strongly favor NGHE Solution, supporting architectural clarity.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed end-to-end with latency measurements and interface monitoring.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of update latency responsibility to NGHE Solution module with defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Monitor NGHE Solution module load to prevent bottlenecks.
Plan fallback or redundancy strategies for telemetry data acquisition.

decision.justification: The functional decomposition, DP selection, and IM assignment meet the latency requirement with clear architectural boundaries and manageable risks. Verification and validation plans are feasible.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Acquire real-time worksite data with minimal delay
FR2: Process and update the worksite model within latency constraints
FR3: Disseminate updated worksite model to relevant modules promptly

revise.why: Current level is sufficiently detailed and consistent; proceed to next decomposition level.

assumptions: Network link supports required bandwidth and latency
Telemetry data is reliable and timely
